mappings.rationaleAI: The Cold War comprehensively covers containment (A.3.a.i), Korean War (A.3.a.iv), Eisenhower's foreign policy (A.3.b.ii), Cuban Missile Crisis (A.4.a), Vietnam War (A.4.b.iii), detente (A.4.d), and Reagan's anticommunist foreign policy through the end of the Cold War (A.4.f.ii).
US foreign policy leading to WWII including lend-lease (A.2.c), US forces defeating the Axis Powers in Europe and the Pacific (A.2.d), and Canadian wartime mobilization and armed forces (B.2).

Canada's interwar Liberal government (B.1), WWII participation (B.2), and post-1945 foreign policy, Commonwealth relations, French-English tensions, and economic prosperity (B.3) are comprehensively covered.
The US from the 1920s Republican administrations through the New Deal, WWII, Cold War, and the end of the Cold War comprehensively covers all of section A.
The stock market crash, Hoover's attempts at economic recovery (A.1.c), and the Great Depression's relief measures in Canada (B.1.b) are directly covered.
US containment of Communism, the Truman Doctrine, and Marshall Plan (A.3.a.i), and US-Soviet relations during the Gorbachev era (A.4.f.ii) are directly covered.

Military aid to South Vietnam under Kennedy (A.4.a), Gulf of Tonkin Resolution and expansion of involvement (A.4.b.iii), continuing Vietnam War and Paris peace talks under Nixon (A.4.c.i), and conclusion of the Vietnam War under Ford (A.4.d).
Creation of NATO as part of containment policy (A.3.a.i) and Canadian participation in NATO and collective security (B.3.a).
North American history from WWI through the postwar boom and Vietnam era covers the full scope of both US (A) and Canadian (B) developments since 1920.
Ford's mass production innovations and the Model T connect to big business favoritism under Harding and Coolidge (A.1.a) and the social conditions of the 1920s (A.1.b).
The Korean War (1950–53) — wartime mobilization and peace treaties with Japan — is directly covered (A.3.a.iv).
US sponsorship of the military coup in Iran (1953) under Eisenhower (A.3.b.ii) and the Iranian hostage crisis under Carter (A.4.e) are directly covered.
US support of Nationalist China (A.3.a.v) and Nixon's rapprochement with the People's Republic of China (A.4.c.i) are directly covered.
Stalin's Soviet regime is the context for the US Truman Doctrine, Marshall Plan, and Cold War containment policy (A.3.a).
Lend-lease aid to Britain (1940–41) is directly covered in US foreign policy between the World Wars (A.2.c).
